key_finding: Neither entropy nor magnitude alone provides adequate detection. Entropy alone performs barely above chance (33.3% accuracy, 3-class random baseline). Magnitude alone improves overall accuracy but is weaker than entropy alone specifically at detecting local (SEAR-style) attacks (F1=0.229 vs 0.387). Combining both features nearly doubles local-attack detection F1 (0.711) relative to either alone, validating the two-feature design as a genuine synergistic improvement rather than an arbitrary choice.
methodological_note: Results show minor variance from Notebook 04's originally reported figures, attributable to GPU non-determinism in backward-pass gradient computation (standard for unseeded cuDNN operations). The three-way comparison within this single run is internally consistent and supports the stated conclusion.
